purpose: Demonstrates the optional image/imageAlt fields added to the question schema (see src/components/QuestionCard.jsx). This question is NOT part of src/content/course.json's live "questions" array and will not appear in the app until it is explicitly promoted there, the same way src/content/imports/import-pack-001.json's questions were promoted only after a separate, deliberate step.
activationRule: Do not add this question to course.json unless its verificationStatus below has been changed to "approved" or "active" AND the placeholder image has been replaced with a real curated photo from Vinci.

questions.prompt: The image shows a lifeboat's capacity/markings plate. What does the "MAX PERSONS" figure on a plate like this represent?
questions.imageAlt: Photo of lifeboat markings from Vinci source batch 2026-07-07

choices.text: The maximum number of persons the lifeboat is certified to carry
The number of crew required to launch the lifeboat
choices.misconception: Confuses the boat's certified passenger capacity with its minimum launch/operating crew, which is a separate figure.

choices.text: The lifeboat's registration or hull serial number
choices.misconception: Confuses a capacity marking with an identification/serial marking, which would appear elsewhere on the plate.

choices.text: The number of times the lifeboat has been inspected
choices.misconception: Confuses a capacity marking with an inspection/maintenance record, which is tracked separately.

hints: This is a safety-critical number posted directly on the boat itself, not a log or maintenance record.
It tells crew and passengers the certified limit for how many people the lifeboat may safely carry.
questions.explanation: Lifeboat capacity/markings plates post the boat's certified maximum number of persons ("MAX PERSONS") so crew never exceed a safe loading limit during an emergency.
questions.sourceReference: Placeholder sample — pending a real curated image and source verification from Vinci
